steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what the hoax entries were “finally recognized as” before being deleted. The passage calls them “fictitious entries” and “hoaxes” — they are made-up falsehoods.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'analyses': An analysis is a careful study or examination. Hoax entries are not studies of anything. Plug in: “recognized as analyses” makes no sense here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'revelations': A revelation is something newly disclosed that is true and surprising. These entries were lies, not truths coming to light. ✗
  • Choice C — 'fabrications': A fabrication is a made-up falsehood. Plug in: “recognized as fabrications” = recognized as invented lies. This matches exactly what fictitious hoax entries are. ✓
  • Choice D — 'enhancements': An enhancement is an improvement. Hoax entries deceived readers; they did not improve Wikipedia. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
